MINI MINUTES:
Vivian Lerche, Esq. specially appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to JAN-08-2019 at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: TERI L. JACKSON; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-23-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Peters, Esq. 888-922-2367 of the Fullman Firm for Defendant Martin Mar through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant cites case. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MARTIN MAR's MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND RECALL QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Martin Mar's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default and default judgment entered against Mr. Mar on November 6, 2013 are set aside. Mr. Mar has shown good cause for setting aside the default and default judgment based on extrinsic fraud as a result of the filing of a proof of service of the summons and complaint on him based on invalid substituted service. Mr. Mar has shown that he did not reside at or have a place of business at the address where he was purportedly substitute served on the date of the purported service and he had no knowledge of this case until September 2017 when his bank account was levied. Mr. Mar acted with reasonable diligence in filing this motion after he learned of the default and default judgment entered against him. Plaintiff Unifund CCR, LLC is required to refund to Mr. Mar $20,542.82, the amount that was taken from Mr. Mar's bank account as a result of a levy on that account by Unifund to collect on the default judgment that has been set aside. Unifund may not make any further efforts to collect on the judgment and all judgment collection documents issued by this court are now invalid. Mr. Mar's request for dismissal of this case due to the failure to serve him within three years of the filing of the case is denied because the time period to serve him is tolled from the date of entry of the default judgment till the date this order is filed. Because Mr. Mar was not validly served, if Unifund wishes to pursue this lawsuit against him, it will need to serve him with a summons and complaint.

Counsel for Mr. Mar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC3.1312.*

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

*After the hearing, order is signed. (see scanned order for details)*

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Heidi Joya, Esq. of The Fullman Firm, T:888.922.2367, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: Counsel for defendant argues that a proof of service was filed. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MARTIN MAR'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND RECALL AND QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION is OFF CALENDAR without prejudice to being refiled with a proper proof of service. No proof of service filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
